what_it_does: Defines a prose-heavy JSON model for sequencing a multi-page design artifact: narrative roles, pitch arc runs, sequence rules, page-count tiers, reading axes, and a generation ladder.
observations: Defines seven narrative roles: open, argue, show, prove, plan, people, close.
Each narrative role includes a plain-language function, archetype affinities, evidence, and a dial envelope for warmth/register/density.
The pitch arc is expressed as ordered role runs with elastic count ranges and a listed-order growth policy.
Rules cover bookends, pacing, title chains, motif recurrence, dial choreography, signal economy, dividers, spreads, and progressive builds.
Tiers map artifact scales from tagline to full deck and describe fusion or extraction strategies for compressed versions.
Reading axes define descent, traverse, root relativity, channel reads, walks, fields, and measure.
The generation ladder stages progress from seed to tokens, with each stage emitting a complete inspectable artifact.
standouts: Treats reading order as a story-tree with vertical descent and horizontal traverse.
Connects sequence planning to page-count compression through fusion, extraction, and conserved story measure.
Frames skimming, drilling in, and navigation as valid walks through the same lattice.
Separates visual warmth/register choreography from layout structure.
conventions: Uses JSON as a design-theory ledger rather than as a compact machine-only config.
Uses prose strings for rules, evidence, status, and rationale.
Uses underscore-prefixed keys for notes, status, verdicts, and growth-policy explanations.
Represents ranges as two-number arrays and role fusions as nested arrays of role names.
Includes dated evidence/status markers inside values.
Uses uppercase emphasis inside prose values for conceptual terms.
concerns: Predicted, derived, future, and evidence-backed statements are mixed in the same structural layer.
Several fields contain long compound prose values that would be difficult to validate mechanically.
Evidence references are string citations rather than structured source objects.
The same file contains planning grammar, reading theory, corpus verdicts, and generation workflow, which creates broad file scope.
notes: This file is a sequence grammar for decks and multi-page artifacts, combining role planning, reading laws, compression tiers, and generation stages.
questions: What exact schema, if any, is expected to validate this prose-heavy JSON?
Should evidence references become structured records with source, date, and location fields?
Are predicted items intended to be consumed differently from verified items?
How should consumers distinguish hard rules from interpretive notes or future work?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated description matches the contents: it presents narrative roles, arcs, story-tree reading axes, tiers, and a generation ladder with evidence and status notes.
apparent_themes: fusion and extraction across scale tiers
complete artifacts at every generation stage
intention_signals: It seems intended to make a repeatable, inspectable grammar for generating and evaluating multi-page design sequences while preserving narrative coherence across scales and reading paths.
novelty: It treats deck sequencing, page compression, reading navigation, visual choreography, and generation workflow as parts of one conserved story lattice.
contribution: Contributes a file-local model of sequence behavior: named narrative roles with envelopes, elastic arc planning, page-count tier compression, reading-axis laws, and a staged generation process that a fused system could retain as sequence semantics.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON design ledger defining narrative roles, elastic pitch arcs, sequence rules, page-count tiers, story-tree reading axes, and a root-first generation ladder for multi-page artifacts.
intention_for_embedding: This was for formalizing how a deck-like artifact is planned, read, compressed, expanded, and generated while keeping each level narratively complete.
